risks: Event Grid is a Tier 2 Dapr binding component in Container Apps, not a native Pub/Sub component — Dapr Pub/Sub integration with Event Grid may require binding configuration instead of pub/sub
No managed identity configured for the Container App to authenticate to Cosmos DB or Event Grid — plan relies on implicit key-based access
Public network access is enabled on both Cosmos DB and Event Grid with no network isolation or private endpoints
Single-region deployment (eastus) with no zone redundancy on Cosmos DB (isZoneRedundant: false) — unsuitable for mission-critical workloads
Placeholder container image (containerapps-helloworld:latest) must be replaced before production use
No Dapr component resources (Microsoft.App/managedEnvironments/daprComponents) defined to wire the Container App to Event Grid and Cosmos DB via Dapr
correctionsRecommended: Add Microsoft.App/managedEnvironments/daprComponents resources for Event Grid binding and Cosmos DB state store to complete the Dapr integration wiring
Add an Event Grid subscription resource (Microsoft.EventGrid/eventSubscriptions) to route events from the topic to the Container App endpoint
Add a user-assigned managed identity with RBAC role assignments for Cosmos DB (Cosmos DB Built-in Data Contributor) and Event Grid (EventGrid Data Sender)
Consider Azure Service Bus or Event Hubs as the Dapr Pub/Sub component instead of Event Grid, since these are Tier 1 supported pub/sub components in Container Apps
Add Azure Key Vault for secrets management rather than relying on Log Analytics shared keys
For production hardening, disable public network access on Cosmos DB and add VNet integration to the Container Apps Environment
